name: Cabezo Cuartal y Nacimiento del río Isuela – circular desde Beratón por la Sierra del Moncayo
region_intro: Hiking around Beratón offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Moncayo Natural Park, characterized by significant elevation changes and varied terrain. The region features prominent peaks like Moncayo, alongside deep ravines and gorges such as Barranco de Castilla and Valcongosto. Trails often traverse forested areas and open mountain slopes, providing a range of hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Beratón The most popular hiking route is Moncayo Circular Trail, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 50 minutes to complete. This route offers a comprehensive exploration of the Moncayo area. Another top favourite among local hikers is Barranco de Castilla Trail, a moderate 4.8 miles (7.8 km) path. This trail navigates through a notable ravine, showcasing the region's geological features. Local hikers also love the Moncayo from Cueva de Ágreda Trail, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain towards Moncayo, often completed in about 4 hours 57 minutes. Hiking around Beratón is defined by its mountainous landscapes, deep ravines, and the natural beauty of the Moncayo Natural Park. The network of trails offers options for easy hikes near Beratón, as well as more challenging ascents for different ability levels. The routes in Beratón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 140 reviews. More than 1,400 hikers have used komoot to explore Beratón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Beratón?
A: There are over 50 hiking routes around Beratón, offering a wide range of experiences from easy strolls to challenging ascents. The area is part of the Moncayo Natural Park, known for its diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Beratón?
A: Yes, Beratón offers 4 easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or families. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances, allowing everyone to enjoy the natural beauty of the Moncayo Natural Park.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I expect to see on hikes around Beratón?
A: Hiking around Beratón allows you to explore prominent peaks like Moncayo, deep ravines such as Barranco de Castilla and Valcongosto, and forested areas. You can also discover interesting natural landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Pilares&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6500103" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Queiles River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Beratón area?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981868281/cabezo-de-la-mata-from-fuente-de-los-frailes-circular-through-the-moncayo-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de la Mata from Fuente de los Frailes — circular through the Moncayo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate challenge. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971965058/valcongosto-gorges-and-cuartun-circular-from-purujosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valcongosto Gorges and Cuartún - circular from Purujosa&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through impressive gorges.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Beratón?
A: The best time to hike in Beratón generally depends on the specific trail and your preferences. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winter brings the possibility of snow, particularly on higher peaks like Moncayo.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Beratón?
A: The hiking routes in Beratón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 140 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the challenge of the mountainous terrain, and the natural beauty of the Moncayo Natural Park.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or panoramic spots recommended for hikers?
A: Many trails in Beratón offer stunning panoramic views, especially those ascending towards Moncayo. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo Summit&lt;/a&gt; itself provides breathtaking vistas of the surrounding region. Trails through open mountain slopes also frequently provide expansive outlooks.

Q: What should I wear for hiking in Beratón?
A: Given the significant elevation changes and varied terrain in the Moncayo Natural Park, it's advisable to wear layers of clothing to adapt to changing weather conditions. Sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support are essential, especially for trails with rocky or uneven surfaces. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

Q: Are there any interesting historical or cultural sites near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can find historical elements like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5249057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Snow Well of Prado de Santa Lucía&lt;/a&gt;. The nearby settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Borobia&lt;/a&gt; also offers a glimpse into the local culture and history.

Q: Are there any facilities like picnic areas or cafes near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, for a break during your hike, you can find facilities such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5249052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Sacristán Picnic Area&lt;/a&gt;. While specific cafes directly on trails might be limited, nearby villages often offer options for refreshments.

Q: What is the difficulty level of hiking trails around Beratón?
A: The trails around Beratón range from easy to difficult. There are 4 easy routes, 38 moderate routes, and 15 difficult routes. This variety ensures options for all fitness levels, from leisurely walks to challenging ascents towards peaks like Moncayo.
tips: At this point we will find an informative poster (very worn) facing the beautiful Muela de Beratón.

Cabezo Cuartal (1,506 m) located in the Sierra del Moncayo, offers us magnificent views of the mountains that surround us such as the Muela de Beratón. The access from the road to the Cabezo is without a path, but we can easily find it walking through the open forest.

At this point, we come across the source of the Isuela River, surrounded by pure nature in the Sierra del Moncayo. In the place, we will find an information panel that will give us more details about the river.

waypoints.name: View of La Muela de Beratón
